extractions.text: Amber body. Brown mane. Head resting on the white wool of the lamb beside it.
extractions.reasoning: Specific visual details of the lion in the stained glass.

extractions.text: The surgery was not omnipotent. It had limits. It could change the instructions, but it could not change the organs.
extractions.reasoning: An extended medical metaphor for the rewriting entity's capabilities and limitations.

extractions.evidence: Text is code. A book is a sequence of instructions.
extractions.text: Dane realizes that text functions as code, a sequence of instructions that can be manipulated.

extractions.evidence: But a painting is not code. A painting is chaos.
extractions.text: Dane concludes that painting embodies chaos, contrasting it with the structured nature of code/text.
